scope: LoRA route evaluation only; no training, model download, API, Demo, Phase 1 or Phase 2 rule changes

data: Only 200 synthetic samples and 160 training samples
All splits share one generator, vocabulary and template family
175 samples are single-anomaly; only 25 contain two or three anomalies
Negative constraints exist in 66 answers but paired hard-negative corrections are absent
Action distribution is 168 request_human_approval and 32 reject, with no auto_approve
prompt_and_gold: Phase 3F improved the written contract but free text still couples copying, coverage, formatting and generation
Low token loss does not enforce exact schema or set equality
Evaluator checks three required sections while Phase 3F gold defines six sections
English enum exact-match requirements are better represented as structured fields
model: Qwen2.5-0.5B is not demonstrated to be stable for strict multi-field factual constraints
4-bit QLoRA may contribute to instability but no controlled precision ablation exists
A larger model may improve compliance but does not solve task definition or evidence binding
evaluation: Phase 3G local per-sample predictions, evaluation and manifest are absent
format_compliance is an all-components gate and cannot alone diagnose the zero score
Regex checks miss implicit claims, paraphrases and unmodeled entity formats
Hallucination rate should be supplemented with violation counts and type distribution
task_definition: Free-text generation is unnecessarily broad when canonical facts and deterministic decisions already exist
The safer task is a schema-constrained explanation plan followed by deterministic rendering

route_matrix.name: Third free-text LoRA
route_matrix.benefit: Tests hard-negative data changes on the same model
route_matrix.risk: Retains the unsafe free-text task and lacks second-run sample diagnostics

route_matrix.name: Structured Output First
route_matrix.benefit: Makes format, copy fields and anomaly coverage directly machine-verifiable
route_matrix.risk: JSON values can still be fabricated without deterministic validation

route_matrix.name: Retrieval-Grounded Explanation
route_matrix.benefit: Makes every explanation bullet traceable to evidence ids
route_matrix.risk: A citation alone does not prove the sentence is entailed

route_matrix.name: Larger Model Or Stronger Decoding
route_matrix.benefit: May improve instruction and format compliance
route_matrix.risk: Higher cost and still requires schema validation, Guard and fallback

required_experiments: Define StructuredExplanation schema and deterministic invariants
Create an independent 20-50 sample challenge set
Run a no-training base-model structured decoding baseline
Add per-field and per-sample evaluator debug output
Consider one structured-output LoRA only if the baseline isolates a trainable failure

phase3h_impact.new_validator_position: between controlled rewrite and existing output guard

next_step_gate.alternative: pause LoRA at the current portfolio stage
